  • Introducing SAP Fiori for Android/iOS 25.8: Innovative Enhancements for Android and iOS.

For an overview of all updates, have a look at the what’s new sections in the SAP Design System portal and the SAP Mobile Services Documentation

  • SAP Fiori for Android design: What's New for SAP BTP SDK for Android 26.1 and older.
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android: What’s New in Release 26.1.2 and earlier releases.
  • SAP Fiori for iOS design: What's New for SAP BTP SDK for iOS 26.1 and older.
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS: What’s New in Version 26.1.2 and earlier releases.

Mobile Development Kit (MDK) News

The Mobile Development Kit, available as part of SAP Mobile Services, enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / VSCode extension) and run it natively on Mobile Android and iOS devices.  

Here I have chosen to highlight one area: enhancements to the calander control, which now supports date range selection as well as allowing you to highlight special events or statuses directly on the calendar.

The new date range selection displays the calendar in a month-view format and allows multiple months to be shown. Users can scroll vertically through the calendar months. This is ideal if you need to plan events or manage schedules over extended periods, since users can select a time period with a start date and an end date (see Figure 6 below):

  • The first date selected becomes the start date of the range.
  • The second date selected becomes the end date of the range.

Figure 6: Date range selection supported by the mobile development kit provided by SAP Mobile Service. Alt Text: An image of an iPhone and an Android phone next to each other. The iPhone with the light Morning Horizon theme and a date selection Sep 16 – Sep 29, 2025, the other with the dark Evening Horizon theme and a date selection Mon Sep 29 to Fri Oct 17 2025.

Find out more here:

  • Blog post: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 25.9.
  • Documentation: What’s New in Mobile Development Kit 25.9.1 and earlier releases.

 

Further Information

For creating web applications, do register for the upcoming UI5con virtual conference.

Learn more about it at https://openui5.org/ui5con/faq.html, including how to attend, sponsor, or propose a topic for a session.

For links to design and development tools and technology, have a look at the SAP Fiori topic page in the SAP Community, or go directly to these pages:

  • SAP Design System.
  • SAP Fiori tools.
  • SAPUI5.
  • SAP Fiori elements.
  • SAP Mobile technologies.

 

Final words

As you can see from all seven posts in the series, we continue to progress rapidly with the user experience of our product portfolio, especially with regards to AI-assistance.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori.
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:  A Key Performance Indicator (KPI) is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its key objectives. In SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs are used to display important business metrics in a clear and visual format. They help users quickly monitor performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

KPI (Key Performance Indicator):  It is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its objectives. KPIs help businesses monitor performance, identify trends, and support data-driven decision-making.

In modern enterprise applications built with SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs provide a quick overview of key business metrics directly within dashboards and analytical applications.

KPI in Analytical List Page:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a powerful Fiori Elements template that combines:

  • KPIs
  • Charts
  • Filters
  • Tables

KPIs are displayed at the Top of the page and help users quickly analyze business data.

Note: In this article, I have not covered the creation of the CDS View, OData service exposure, or the application creation in SAP BTP, as these topics were already explained in my previous blogs. In this post, I focus only on providing an example of the KPI annotations and explaining where these annotations are added in the project.

CDS:

You can add the KPI annotations in the Metadata Extension file. This approach helps keep UI-related annotations separate from the core CDS view and allows developers to manage UI configurations more efficiently.

KPI Annotation:

.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]

Once the annotations are added in the Metadata Extension, you can configure the KPI annotation in the manifest.json file so that it is recognized and displayed in the Analytical List Page application.

qualifier: 'KPIAMT'
 

"keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }

Path:

sap.ui.generic.app → pages → AnalyticalListPage → component → settings → keyPerformanceIndicators
 "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },

Note : After that, ensure that the model used for the KPI configuration is also created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name defined for the KPI must be maintained in the models configuration so that the application can correctly retrieve the KPI data from the OData service.

The model name used in the KPI configuration (for example, KPI1) must also be created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name should be maintained .

Path:

sap.ui5 → models → KPI1

    "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
"models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },

Once all the configurations are completed, the KPI is successfully created. You can see the KPI displayed in the Analytical List Page as shown in the screenshot below for your reference.

Full Codes: This code example covers the Analytical List Page, including the configuration of Visual Filters and KPI annotations used in the application.

1.CDS View:
-------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
___________________________________
2.Meta data Extension:
------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]


annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.title: 'Total Amount'
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}
__________________________________
3. Annotation.xml file:
-----------------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>
 _____________________________          
4. Manifest.json File:
----------------------------------------------
{
    "_version": "1.82.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "analyticalreport",
        "type": "application",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "0.0.1"
        },
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "resources": "resources.json",
        "sourceTemplate": {
            "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:alp",
            "version": "1.20.4",
            "toolsId": "530787e5-fdb9-4d9d-87bf-ca33db19cf1d"
        },
        "dataSources": {
            "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value/",
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL.xml"
                }
            },
            "annotation": {
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
                }
            },
            "mainService": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/",
                "type": "OData",
                "settings": {
                    "annotations": [
                        "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL",
                        "annotation"
                    ],
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
                    "odataVersion": "2.0"
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.ui": {
        "technology": "UI5",
        "icons": {
            "icon": "",
            "favIcon": "",
            "phone": "",
            "phone@2": "",
            "tablet": "",
            "tablet@2": ""
        },
        "deviceTypes": {
            "desktop": true,
            "tablet": true,
            "phone": true
        }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "flexEnabled": true,
        "dependencies": {
            "minUI5Version": "1.145.0",
            "libs": {
                "sap.m": {},
                "sap.ui.core": {},
                "sap.ushell": {},
                "sap.f": {},
                "sap.ui.comp": {},
                "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
                "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
            }
        },
        "contentDensities": {
            "compact": true,
            "cozy": true
        },
        "models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
            "@i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
            }
        },
        "resources": {
            "css": []
        },
        "routing": {
            "config": {},
            "routes": [],
            "targets": {}
        }
    },
    "sap.ui.generic.app": {
        "_version": "1.3.0",
        "settings": {
            "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
            "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
            "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
            "showDraftToggle": false
        },
        "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "pages": {
                    "ObjectPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                        "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                        "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
                        "component": {
                            "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.fiori": {
        "registrationIds": [],
        "archeType": "analytical"
    }
}

 

Conclusion : In conclusion, KPIs in SAP Fiori Elements provide a quick and effective way to monitor critical business metrics. By presenting key values in a visual and easy-to-understand format, KPIs help users track performance and take timely actions to improve business outcomes.

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: How to Create an Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction : The Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a powerful floorplan designed for data analysis and monitoring business performance. It combines visual charts, visual filters, and a data table within a single page, allowing users to analyze large amounts of data efficiently. The Analytical List Page enables users to quickly identify trends, filter information, and drill down into detailed data for better business insights.

Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a SAP Fiori Elements floorplan designed for data analysis and reporting. It combines charts, filters, and tables in a single page so users can analyze business data interactively.

ALP allows users to visualize, filter, and drill down into data without creating custom UI code.

Key Features of Analytical List Page:

  1.  Visual Filter--->The filter bar allows users to filter data using graphical representations.
  2. Analytical Charts--->Charts provide a visual representation of aggregated business data.
  3. Data Table--->The table shows detailed records corresponding to the chart data.
  4. Interactive Filtering--->When a user clicks a chart segment, the table automatically filters the related records.

Flow of  Analytical List Page:

  1. CDS View
  2. Annotation
  3. Odata service
  4. SAP Fiori Elements ( Analytical List Page) Template
  5. SAP Fiori Application UI

Step 1: Create CDS View and Metadata Extension

First, create the Core Data Services (CDS) view that exposes the required business data.
After creating the CDS view, create a Metadata Extension and add the necessary UI annotations to define how the data should appear on the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page.

The metadata extension is used to separate the UI annotations from the main CDS view, which helps maintain a cleaner and more structured development approach.

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to create a CDS View and a Metadata Extension in the ABAP environment. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step creation process. Instead, the examples below directly demonstrate the CDS view and Metadata Extension with the required annotations used for the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page.

1. CDS View:

Code:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}

2. Meta data Extension:

 

Code:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }
   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
   
     
}]



annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}

 

Step 2: Consume the CDS View in an OData Service

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to consume a CDS View in an OData service. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step process. Instead, the explanation below directly continues with the required configuration and examples.

Go to SEGW Tcode and open your Project and   Add Your CDS View.

After adding the CDS entity, the Entity Type, Entity Set, and related properties will be generated automatically in the project.

Step 3:  Create SAP Fiori Application using Analytical List Page Template

Follow the steps below:

Open BAS  and Click  on File  and Select as New Project from Template  Chose  SAP Fiori  Generator Template under the Template Select as Analytical List Page and  Provide  Required Details and Finish it.

After completing these steps, the system automatically generates the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page  application.

 

Once all the above steps are completed and the configuration is finished, preview the application in SAP Business Application Studio. The application will load successfully, and you will be able to see the Analytical List Page with the data displayed in charts and tables.

In the CDS annotations, I have configured the chart type as a Pie Chart by default. For this reason, the data is displayed as a Pie Chart in the above screenshot. However, the chart type can be changed based on the client's business requirements.
For Your Reference: This annotation has already been added in the Metadata Extension View.

Depending on the client’s requirements, you can change the chart type in the Fiori Elements application to formats such as Pie Chart, Column Chart, Bar Chart, or Line Chart. Please see the screenshot below for reference.

 

 

Conclusion: In conclusion, the Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides an effective way to analyze and monitor business data through interactive charts, visual filters, and tables. By combining data visualization with filtering capabilities, it helps users gain insights quickly and make informed decisions more efficiently.

 

Please go below Links:

 Visual Filter: https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/kpi-key-performance-indicator-a-simple-guide/ba-p/14348850 

KPI(Key Performance Indicator): //community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/visual-filters-in-sap-fiori-elements-a-complete-guide/ba-p/14348265 

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Overview

If you are integrating your Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone, you may face

the challenge of exposing derived and composite roles containing launchpad content.

Creating (PFCG) roles on ABAP system, that derive from roles with launchpad content, is mainly driven by the need to differentiate authorization for the same set of applications. This often leads to many roles deriving from the same base role. In such cases, the derived roles are assigned to users instead of their base roles. The derived roles get the content of the role menu, like Fiori catalogs or spaces from inheritance and focus mainly on authorizations and user assignments.

This blog outlines the approach for exposing derived roles from an ABAP system to SAP Build Work Zone.

Related Blog

In the blog How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone you will learn two options when exposing composite roles. The recommended approach for integrating derived roles is the same as outlined in “Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)” when exposing composite roles.

Integrating Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone

When exposing derived roles to SAP Build Work Zone there is a separation of concerns between the exposure of launchpad content, authorization and role-to-user assignment.

For the exposure, you select only the single roles that contain launchpad content in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE. Derived roles are not supported in the exposure report. This restriction prevents exposing a high number of roles with identical launchpad content. When the content for exposure is processed, the system identifies roles which derive from the selected roles and includes these references in the exposed CDM content (as relatedRoleAssignments). These references are not exposed to the administrator during exposure but used by the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) and SAP Build Work Zone infrastructure.

 

Identity Provisioning Service and SAP Build Work Zone handle the role assignment logic, ensuring that users with derived roles on the ABAP side get the correct roles assigned on the BTP side. With that, users get access to the application they are authorized for.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about roles deriving from it and their relationships, but those derived roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The IPS ensures that when an user is assigned to a derived role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information, see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • This approach is supported since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022 and later.

 

Conclusion 

Integrating derived roles with SAP Build Work Zone works best with IPS-driven provisioning, as it preserves existing role structure and automated user to role assignment. Without IPS one must manually ensure that corresponding roles in both BTP and ABAP system are assigned, which is impractical in larger scenarios. The IPS based approach also supports the integration of composite roles, which are often used together with derived roles.  In that case only single roles should be exposed. Information about related derived or composite roles is provided though CDM content and processed by SAP Build Work Zone and IPS.

 

Further Information

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Restrictions

entry.title: Removal of legacy sap.ui.webc.* Web Component UI libraries from SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.147.0 onwards
entry.content:
Saying Goodbye to sap.ui.webc.* — How SAPUI5/OpenUI5 Now Integrates Web Components Natively

If you've used the sap.ui.webc.main, sap.ui.webc.fiori, or sap.ui.webc.common libraries in a SAPUI5 or OpenUI5 application, this post is for you. These libraries will be removed in version 1.147.0, one release before the next long-term support (LTS) version 1.148. They will not be available in the 1.148 LTS or any version after.

Here's what happened, what's changing, and what you should do.

The Short Version

The sap.ui.webc.* libraries were an experimental attempt to bring UI5 Web Components into the SAPUI5 programming model. They were introduced in version 1.92 (2021) as experimental, deprecated in version 1.120 (2023), and will be removed in version 1.147.0. They never left experimental status.

The new solution is a much better approach: native Web Components integration built directly into sap.ui.core, available since version 1.138. Instead of frozen wrapper libraries, you now consume Web Components from npm as first-class citizens of the framework.

What Were the sap.ui.webc.* Libraries?

The idea was straightforward: take each UI5 Web Component (like ui5-button, ui5-shellbar, ui5-table) and wrap it as a classic SAPUI5 ManagedObject-based control — complete with properties, aggregations, events, and data binding support.

Three libraries made up this approach:

Library Purpose

sap.ui.webc.commonBase infrastructure, bundled the UI5 Web Components runtime
sap.ui.webc.main~70 controls wrapping @ui5/webcomponents (Button, Input, Table, Dialog, ...)
sap.ui.webc.fiori~30 controls wrapping @ui5/webcomponents-fiori (ShellBar, Wizard, Timeline, ...)

This "retrofit" approach worked, but it came with significant drawbacks:

  • Frozen dependency. The libraries bundled @ui5/webcomponents v1.18.0 as static thirdparty files. When the UI5 Web Components project shipped v2.x with major improvements, the sap.ui.webc.* libraries stayed on v1.18 — forever.
  • Maintenance overhead. Every new Web Component or API change required manually updating the wrapper code and re-bundling the thirdparty files. This didn't scale.
  • Experimental lock. Because of these constraints, the libraries never graduated beyond @experimental status. They were always flagged with: "This API is experimental and might change significantly."

Why They Were Deprecated

In version 1.120 — the previous LTS release — all three libraries were marked @deprecated with the message:

"This Web Components consumption model has been discarded. An alternative consumption model is planned for the future."

That alternative has arrived a while ago.

The Better Approach: sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent

Since version 1.138, SAPUI5 includes a new public base class — sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent — that lives directly in sap.ui.core. This is not another wrapper library. It's a framework-level integration point.

Here's what's different:

1. Web Components come from npm, not from frozen bundles.

Instead of shipping a static copy of @ui5/webcomponents, the new approach uses ui5-tooling-modules to consume Web Components directly from npm packages at build time. This means your application stays on current Web Component versions — today v2.x — rather than being locked to a frozen v1.18 copy.

2. Integration is at the framework level.

The WebComponent base class in sap.ui.core handles property propagation, aggregation-to-slot mapping, event forwarding, and rendering integration generically. Individual Web Component wrappers are thin — generated metadata, not hand-maintained control code.

3. The framework is already using it internally.

The new integration approach is already used within the framework itself — for example, the Shell and Enterprise Search in the sap.f library consume Web Components through this mechanism. While these specific integrations are currently private/restricted APIs, they validate that the approach works at scale in production scenarios. Public Web Component-based controls built on sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent will follow.

4. It's public API.

Unlike the sap.ui.webc.* libraries which stayed experimental for their entire lifecycle, sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent is a public, stable API since 1.138.

What You Should Do

If you never used sap.ui.webc.*: Nothing changes for you. If you want to use Web Components in your SAPUI5 app going forward, read the Using Web Components documentation.

If you used sap.ui.webc.* in a prototype or experiment: Since these libraries were always experimental, now is the time to move to the new approach. The controls you were using (like sap.ui.webc.main.Button or sap.ui.webc.fiori.ShellBar) have no 1:1 migration path because the consumption model changed fundamentally. However, the underlying Web Components are the same — and better, since you now get v2.x instead of v1.18.

If you used sap.ui.webc.* in a productive application despite the experimental status: The libraries will be removed in version 1.147.0. We recommend starting the migration now. The Using Web Components guide is your starting point.

If you're on the 1.120 LTS and planning to upgrade to the next LTS: The previous LTS (1.120) still ships the deprecated sap.ui.webc.* libraries. The next LTS (1.148) will not — they are removed one version earlier in 1.147. If your application depends on these libraries, you must migrate before upgrading to 1.148 LTS.

Timeline

Version Event

1.92 (2021)sap.ui.webc.* introduced as experimental
1.120 (2023)sap.ui.webc.* deprecated — previous LTS
1.138 (2025)sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent introduced as public API
1.147sap.ui.webc.* libraries removed
1.148Next LTS release — sap.ui.webc.* not available

Summary

The sap.ui.webc.* libraries were an early experiment that taught us a lot about what Web Components integration in SAPUI5 should look like. The answer turned out to be: don't bundle frozen copies — integrate at the framework level and let developers consume Web Components from the ecosystem directly.

That's exactly what the new approach does, and it's available today.

Next step: Using Web Components in SAPUI5
